Defaults to all scopes if not specified. */\n    10→  scope?: OptInFeatureScope[];\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→/** All available scopes for feature opt-in configuration */\n    14→export const ALL_SCOPES: OptInFeatureScope[] = [\"org\", \"team\", \"user\"];\n    15→\n    16→/**\n    17→ * Features that appear in opt-in settings.\n    18→ * Add new features here to make them available for user/team opt-in.\n    19→ */\n    20→export const OPT_IN_FEATURES: OptInFeatureConfig[] = [\n    21→  // Example - to be populated with actual features\n    22→  // {\n    23→  //   slug: \"bookings-v3\",\n    24→  //   titleI18nKey: \"bookings_v3_title\",\n    25→  //   descriptionI18nKey: \"bookings_v3_description\",\n    26→  //   policy: \"permissive\",\n    27→  //   scope: [\"org\", \"team\", \"user\"], // Optional: defaults to all scopes if not specified\n    28→  // },\n    29→];\n    30→\n    31→/**\n    32→ * Get the configuration for a specific opt-in feature by slug.\n    33→ */\n    34→export function getOptInFeatureConfig(slug: string): OptInFeatureConfig | undefined {\n    35→  return OPT_IN_FEATURES.find((f) => f.slug === slug);\n    36→}\n    37→\n    38→/**\n    39→ * Check if a slug is in the opt-in allowlist.\n    40→ * Acts as a type guard, narrowing the slug to FeatureId when true.\n    41→ */\n    42→export function isOptInFeature(slug: string): slug is FeatureId {\n    43→  return OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => f.slug === slug);\n    44→}\n    45→\n    46→/**\n    47→ * Check if there are any opt-in features available.\n    48→ */\n    49→export const HAS_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.length > 0;\n    50→\n    51→/** Whether there are opt-in features available for the user scope */\n    52→export const HAS_USER_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\"user\"));\n    53→\n    54→/** Whether there are opt-in features available for the team scope */\n    55→export const HAS_TEAM_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\"team\"));\n    56→\n    57→/** Whether there are opt-in features available for the org scope */\n    58→export const HAS_ORG_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\"org\"));\n    59→\n    60→/**\n    61→ * Get opt-in features that are available for a specific scope.\n    62→ * Features without a scope field are available for all scopes.\n    63→ */\n    64→export function getOptInFeaturesForScope(scope: OptInFeatureScope): OptInFeatureConfig[] {\n    65→  return OPT_IN_FEATURES.filter((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(scope));\n    66→}\n    67→\n    68→/**\n    69→ * Check if a feature is allowed for a specific scope.\n    70→ * Features without a scope field are allowed for all scopes.\n    71→ * Features not in the config are NOT allowed (must be explicitly configured).\n    72→ */\n    73→export function isFeatureAllowedForScope(slug: string, scope: OptInFeatureScope): boolean {\n    74→  const config = getOptInFeatureConfig(slug);\n    75→  if (!config) return false;\n    76→  return config.scope.includes(scope);\n    77→}\n    78→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f9ff3b5b-9f3c-4d46-a962-edfd64b40be7","uuid":"ab252d81-e90b-4086-9b20-eb441e0d889a","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/workspace/packages/features/feature-opt-in/config.ts","content":"import type { FeatureId } from \"@calcom/features/flags/config\";\nimport type { OptInFeaturePolicy, OptInFeatureScope } from \"./types\";\n\nexport interface OptInFeatureConfig {\n  slug: FeatureId;\n  titleI18nKey: string;\n  descriptionI18nKey: string;\n  policy: OptInFeaturePolicy;\n  /** Scopes where this feature can be configured. Defaults to all scopes if not specified. */\n  scope?: OptInFeatureScope[];\n}\n\n/** All available scopes for feature opt-in configuration */\nexport const ALL_SCOPES: OptInFeatureScope[] = [\"org\", \"team\", \"user\"];\n\n/**\n * Features that appear in opt-in settings.\n * Add new features here to make them available for user/team opt-in.\n */\nexport const OPT_IN_FEATURES: OptInFeatureConfig[] = [\n  // Example - to be populated with actual features\n  // {\n  //   slug: \"bookings-v3\",\n  //   titleI18nKey: \"bookings_v3_title\",\n  //   descriptionI18nKey: \"bookings_v3_description\",\n  //   policy: \"permissive\",\n  //   scope: [\"org\", \"team\", \"user\"], // Optional: defaults to all scopes if not specified\n  // },\n];\n\n/**\n * Get the configuration for a specific opt-in feature by slug.\n */\nexport function getOptInFeatureConfig(slug: string): OptInFeatureConfig | undefined {\n  return OPT_IN_FEATURES.find((f) => f.slug === slug);\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a slug is in the opt-in allowlist.\n * Acts as a type guard, narrowing the slug to FeatureId when true.\n */\nexport function isOptInFeature(slug: string): slug is FeatureId {\n  return OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => f.slug === slug);\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if there are any opt-in features available.\n */\nexport const HAS_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.length > 0;\n\n/** Whether there are opt-in features available for the user scope */\nexport const HAS_USER_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\"user\"));\n\n/** Whether there are opt-in features available for the team scope */\nexport const HAS_TEAM_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\"team\"));\n\n/** Whether there are opt-in features available for the org scope */\nexport const HAS_ORG_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\"org\"));\n\n/**\n * Get opt-in features that are available for a specific scope.\n * Features without a scope field are available for all scopes.\n */\nexport function getOptInFeaturesForScope(scope: OptInFeatureScope): OptInFeatureConfig[] {\n  return OPT_IN_FEATURES.filter((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(scope));\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a feature is allowed for a specific scope.\n * Features without a scope field are allowed for all scopes.\n * Features not in the config are NOT allowed (must be explicitly configured).\n */\nexport function isFeatureAllowedForScope(slug: string, scope: OptInFeatureScope): boolean {\n  const config = getOptInFeatureConfig(slug);\n  if (!config) return false;\n  return config.scope.includes(scope);\n}\n","numLines":78,"startLine":1,"totalLines":78}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Xh4W86A9eWiTdNyPcpn2Yd","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import type { FeatureId, FeatureState } from \"@calcom/features/flags/config\";\n     2→import type { FeaturesRepository } from \"@calcom/features/flags/features.repository\";\n     3→import { ErrorCode } from \"@calcom/lib/errorCodes\";\n     4→import { ErrorWithCode } from \"@calcom/lib/errors\";\n     5→\n     6→import { getOptInFeatureConfig, getOptInFeaturesForScope, isFeatureAllowedForScope } from \"../config\";\n     7→import { applyAutoOptIn } from \"../lib/applyAutoOptIn\";\n     8→import { computeEffectiveStateAcrossTeams } from \"../lib/computeEffectiveState\";\n     9→import type { OptInFeaturePolicy, OptInFeatureScope } from \"../types\";\n    10→import type {\n    11→  EffectiveStateReason,\n    12→  IFeatureOptInService,\n    13→  ResolvedFeatureState,\n    14→} from \"./IFeatureOptInService\";\n    15→\n    16→type ListFeaturesForUserResult = {\n    17→  featureId: FeatureId;\n    18→  globalEnabled: boolean;\n    19→  orgState: FeatureState;\n    20→  teamStates: FeatureState[];\n    21→  userState: FeatureState | undefined;\n    22→  effectiveEnabled: boolean;\n    23→  effectiveReason: EffectiveStateReason;\n    24→  orgAutoOptIn: boolean;\n    25→  teamAutoOptIns: boolean[];\n    26→  userAutoOptIn: boolean;\n    27→};\n    28→\n    29→type ListFeaturesForTeamResult = {\n    30→  featureId: FeatureId;\n    31→  globalEnabled: boolean;\n    32→  teamState: FeatureState;\n    33→  orgState: FeatureState;\n    34→};\n    35→\n    36→function getOrgState(orgId: number | null, teamStatesById: Record<number, FeatureState>): FeatureState {\n    37→  if (orgId !== null) {\n    38→    return teamStatesById[orgId] ?? \"inherit\";\n    39→  }\n    40→  return \"inherit\";\n    41→}\n    42→\n    43→function getOrgAutoOptIn(orgId: number | null, teamsAutoOptIn: Record<number, boolean>): boolean {\n    44→  if (orgId !== null) {\n    45→    return teamsAutoOptIn[orgId] ?? false;\n    46→  }\n    47→  return false;\n    48→}\n    49→\n    50→function getTeamIdsToQuery(teamId: number, parentOrgId: number | null | undefined): number[] {\n    51→  if (parentOrgId) {\n    52→    return [teamId, parentOrgId];\n    53→  }\n    54→  return [teamId];\n    55→}\n    56→\n    57→function getOrgStateForTeam(\n    58→  parentOrgId: number | null | undefined,\n    59→  teamStates: Record<string, Record<number, FeatureState>>,\n    60→  slug: FeatureId\n    61→): FeatureState {\n    62→  if (parentOrgId) {\n    63→    return teamStates[slug]?.[parentOrgId] ?? \"inherit\";\n    64→  }\n    65→  return \"inherit\";\n    66→}\n    67→\n    68→/**\n    69→ * Service class for managing feature opt-in logic.\n    70→ * Computes effective states based on global, org, team, and user settings.\n    71→ */\n    72→export class FeatureOptInService implements IFeatureOptInService {\n    73→  constructor(private featuresRepository: FeaturesRepository) {}\n    74→\n    75→  /**\n    76→   * Core method: Resolve feature states for a user across all their teams.\n    77→   *\n    78→   * Precedence rules:\n    79→   * 1. Global disabled → false (stop)\n    80→   * 2. Org explicitly disabled → false (stop)\n    81→   * 3. Org explicitly enabled → allowed at org level, continue to teams\n    82→   * 4. Org inherits (or no org) → check teams\n    83→   * 5. All teams explicitly disabled → false (stop)\n    84→   * 6. At least one team enabled OR inherits → allowed at team level, continue to user\n    85→   * 7. User explicitly disabled → false\n    86→   * 8. User explicitly enabled OR inherits → true\n    87→   *\n    88→   * Auto-opt-in transformation:\n    89→   * - If autoOptInFeatures=true at a level AND state is \"inherit\", transform to \"enabled\"\n    90→   * - This transformation happens before computing effectiveEnabled\n    91→   */\n    92→  async resolveFeatureStatesAcrossTeams({\n    93→    userId,\n    94→    orgId,\n    95→    teamIds,\n    96→    featureIds,\n    97→  }: {\n    98→    userId: number;\n    99→    orgId: number | null;\n   100→    teamIds: number[];\n   101→    featureIds: FeatureId[];\n   102→  }): Promise<Record<string, ResolvedFeatureState>> {\n   103→    const allTeamIds = orgId !== null ? [orgId, ...teamIds] : teamIds;\n   104→\n   105→    const [allFeatures, allTeamStates, userStates, userAutoOptIn, teamsAutoOptIn] = await Promise.all([\n   106→      this.featuresRepository.getAllFeatures(),\n   107→      this.featuresRepository.getTeamsFeatureStates({ teamIds: allTeamIds, featureIds }),\n   108→      this.featuresRepository.getUserFeatureStates({ userId, featureIds }),\n   109→      this.featuresRepository.getUserAutoOptIn(userId),\n   110→      this.featuresRepository.getTeamsAutoOptIn(allTeamIds),\n   111→    ]);\n   112→\n   113→    const globalEnabledMap = new Map(allFeatures.map((feature) => [feature.slug, feature.enabled ?? false]));\n   114→    const resolvedStates: Record<string, ResolvedFeatureState> = {};\n   115→\n   116→    for (const featureId of featureIds) {\n   117→      const state = this.resolveFeatureState(\n   118→        featureId,\n   119→        orgId,\n   120→        teamIds,\n   121→        globalEnabledMap,\n   122→        allTeamStates,\n   123→        userStates,\n   124→        teamsAutoOptIn,\n   125→        userAutoOptIn\n   126→      );\n   127→      resolvedStates[featureId] = state;\n   128→    }\n   129→\n   130→    return resolvedStates;\n   131→  }\n   132→\n   133→  private resolveFeatureState(\n   134→    featureId: FeatureId,\n   135→    orgId: number | null,\n   136→    teamIds: number[],\n   137→    globalEnabledMap: Map<string, boolean>,\n   138→    allTeamStates: Record<string, Record<number, FeatureState>>,\n   139→    userStates: Record<string, FeatureState>,\n   140→    teamsAutoOptIn: Record<number, boolean>,\n   141→    userAutoOptIn: boolean\n   142→  ): ResolvedFeatureState {\n   143→    const globalEnabled = globalEnabledMap.get(featureId) ?? false;\n   144→    const teamStatesById = allTeamStates[featureId] ?? {};\n   145→\n   146→    const orgState = getOrgState(orgId, teamStatesById);\n   147→    const teamStates = teamIds.map((teamId) => teamStatesById[teamId] ?? \"inherit\");\n   148→    const userState = userStates[featureId] ?? \"inherit\";\n   149→    const orgAutoOptIn = getOrgAutoOptIn(orgId, teamsAutoOptIn);\n   150→    const teamAutoOptIns = teamIds.map((teamId) => teamsAutoOptIn[teamId] ?? false);\n   151→\n   152→    const { effectiveOrgState, effectiveTeamStates, effectiveUserState } = applyAutoOptIn({\n   153→      orgState,\n   154→      teamStates,\n   155→      userState,\n   156→      orgAutoOptIn,\n   157→      teamAutoOptIns,\n   158→      userAutoOptIn,\n   159→    });\n   160→\n   161→    const policy: OptInFeaturePolicy = \"permissive\";\n   162→\n   163→    const { enabled: effectiveEnabled, reason: effectiveReason } = computeEffectiveStateAcrossTeams({\n   164→      globalEnabled,\n   165→      orgState: effectiveOrgState,\n   166→      teamStates: effectiveTeamStates,\n   167→      userState: effectiveUserState,\n   168→      policy,\n   169→    });\n   170→\n   171→    return {\n   172→      featureId,\n   173→      globalEnabled,\n   174→      orgState,\n   175→      teamStates,\n   176→      userState,\n   177→      effectiveEnabled,\n   178→      effectiveReason,\n   179→      orgAutoOptIn,\n   180→      teamAutoOptIns,\n   181→      userAutoOptIn,\n   182→    };\n   183→  }\n   184→\n   185→  /**\n   186→   * List all opt-in features with their states for a user across teams.\n   187→   * Only returns features that are in the allowlist, globally enabled, and scoped to \"user\".\n   188→   */\n   189→  async listFeaturesForUser(input: {\n   190→    userId: number;\n   191→    orgId: number | null;\n   192→    teamIds: number[];\n   193→  }): Promise<ListFeaturesForUserResult[]> {\n   194→    const { userId, orgId, teamIds } = input;\n   195→    const userScopedFeatures = getOptInFeaturesForScope(\"user\");\n   196→    const featureIds = userScopedFeatures.map((config) => config.slug);\n   197→\n   198→    const resolvedStates = await this.resolveFeatureStatesAcrossTeams({\n   199→      userId,\n   200→      orgId,\n   201→      teamIds,\n   202→      featureIds,\n   203→    });\n   204→\n   205→    return featureIds.map((featureId) => resolvedStates[featureId]).filter((state) => !state.globalEnabled);\n   206→  }\n   207→\n   208→  /**\n   209→   * List all opt-in features with their raw states for a team or organization.\n   210→   * Used for team/org admin settings page to configure feature opt-in.\n   211→   * Only returns features that are in the allowlist, globally enabled, and scoped to the specified scope.\n   212→   * If parentOrgId is provided, also returns the organization state for each feature.\n   213→   */\n   214→  async listFeaturesForTeam(input: {\n   215→    teamId: number;\n   216→    parentOrgId?: number | null;\n   217→    scope?: OptInFeatureScope;\n   218→  }): Promise<ListFeaturesForTeamResult[]> {\n   219→    const { teamId, parentOrgId, scope = \"team\" } = input;\n   220→    const teamIdsToQuery = getTeamIdsToQuery(teamId, parentOrgId);\n   221→    const scopedFeatures = getOptInFeaturesForScope(scope);\n   222→\n   223→    const [allFeatures, teamStates] = await Promise.all([\n   224→      this.featuresRepository.getAllFeatures(),\n   225→      this.featuresRepository.getTeamsFeatureStates({\n   226→        teamIds: teamIdsToQuery,\n   227→        featureIds: scopedFeatures.map((config) => config.slug),\n   228→      }),\n   229→    ]);\n   230→\n   231→    const results = scopedFeatures.map((config) => {\n   232→      const globalFeature = allFeatures.find((f) => f.slug === config.slug);\n   233→      const globalEnabled = globalFeature?.enabled ?? false;\n   234→      const teamState = teamStates[config.slug]?.[teamId] ?? \"inherit\";\n   235→      const orgState = getOrgStateForTeam(parentOrgId, teamStates, config.slug);\n   236→\n   237→      return { featureId: config.slug, globalEnabled, teamState, orgState };\n   238→    });\n   239→\n   240→    return results.filter((result) => result.globalEnabled);\n   241→  }\n   242→\n   243→  /**\n   244→   * Set user's feature state.\n   245→   * Delegates to FeaturesRepository.setUserFeatureState.\n   246→   * Throws an error if the feature is not scoped to \"user\".\n   247→   */\n   248→  async setUserFeatureState(\n   249→    input:\n   250→      | { userId: number; featureId: FeatureId; state: \"enabled\" | \"disabled\"; assignedBy: number }\n   251→      | { userId: number; featureId: FeatureId; state: \"inherit\" }\n   252→  ): Promise<void> {\n   253→    const { userId, featureId, state } = input;\n   254→\n   255→    if (!isFeatureAllowedForScope(featureId, \"user\")) {\n   256→      throw new ErrorWithCode(\n   257→        ErrorCode.BadRequest,\n   258→        `Feature \"${featureId}\" is not available at the user scope`\n   259→      );\n   260→    }\n   261→\n   262→    if (state === \"inherit\") {\n   263→      await this.featuresRepository.setUserFeatureState({ userId, featureId, state });\n   264→    } else {\n   265→      const { assignedBy } = input;\n   266→      await this.featuresRepository.setUserFeatureState({\n   267→        userId,\n   268→        featureId,\n   269→        state,\n   270→        assignedBy: `user-${assignedBy}`,\n   271→      });\n   272→    }\n   273→  }\n   274→\n   275→  /**\n   276→   * Set team's feature state.\n   277→   * Delegates to FeaturesRepository.setTeamFeatureState.\n   278→   * Throws an error if the feature is not scoped to the specified scope.\n   279→   */\n   280→  async setTeamFeatureState(\n   281→    input:\n   282→      | { teamId: number; featureId: FeatureId; state: \"enabled\" | \"disabled\"; assignedBy: number; scope?: OptInFeatureScope }\n   283→      | { teamId: number; featureId: FeatureId; state: \"inherit\"; scope?: OptInFeatureScope }\n   284→  ): Promise<void> {\n   285→    const { teamId, featureId, state } = input;\n   286→    const scope = input.scope ?? \"team\";\n   287→\n   288→    if (!isFeatureAllowedForScope(featureId, scope)) {\n   289→      throw new ErrorWithCode(\n   290→        ErrorCode.BadRequest,\n   291→        `Feature \"${featureId}\" is not available at the ${scope} scope`\n   292→      );\n   293→    }\n   294→\n   295→    if (state === \"inherit\") {\n   296→      await this.featuresRepository.setTeamFeatureState({ teamId, featureId, state });\n   297→    } else {\n   298→      const { assignedBy } = input;\n   299→      await this.featuresRepository.setTeamFeatureState({\n   300→        teamId,\n   301→        featureId,\n   302→        state,\n   303→        assignedBy: `user-${assignedBy}`,\n   304→      });\n   305→ You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f9ff3b5b-9f3c-4d46-a962-edfd64b40be7","uuid":"1296a89c-0ccd-438f-897b-8a4a792a07a3","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/workspace/packages/features/feature-opt-in/services/FeatureOptInService.ts","content":"import type { FeatureId, FeatureState } from \"@calcom/features/flags/config\";\nimport type { FeaturesRepository } from \"@calcom/features/flags/features.repository\";\nimport { ErrorCode } from \"@calcom/lib/errorCodes\";\nimport { ErrorWithCode } from \"@calcom/lib/errors\";\n\nimport { getOptInFeatureConfig, getOptInFeaturesForScope, isFeatureAllowedForScope } from \"../config\";\nimport { applyAutoOptIn } from \"../lib/applyAutoOptIn\";\nimport { computeEffectiveStateAcrossTeams } from \"../lib/computeEffectiveState\";\nimport type { OptInFeaturePolicy, OptInFeatureScope } from \"../types\";\nimport type {\n  EffectiveStateReason,\n  IFeatureOptInService,\n  ResolvedFeatureState,\n} from \"./IFeatureOptInService\";\n\ntype ListFeaturesForUserResult = {\n  featureId: FeatureId;\n  globalEnabled: boolean;\n  orgState: FeatureState;\n  teamStates: FeatureState[];\n  userState: FeatureState | undefined;\n  effectiveEnabled: boolean;\n  effectiveReason: EffectiveStateReason;\n  orgAutoOptIn: boolean;\n  teamAutoOptIns: boolean[];\n  userAutoOptIn: boolean;\n};\n\ntype ListFeaturesForTeamResult = {\n  featureId: FeatureId;\n  globalEnabled: boolean;\n  teamState: FeatureState;\n  orgState: FeatureState;\n};\n\nfunction getOrgState(orgId: number | null, teamStatesById: Record<number, FeatureState>): FeatureState {\n  if (orgId !== null) {\n    return teamStatesById[orgId] ?? \"inherit\";\n  }\n  return \"inherit\";\n}\n\nfunction getOrgAutoOptIn(orgId: number | null, teamsAutoOptIn: Record<number, boolean>): boolean {\n  if (orgId !== null) {\n    return teamsAutoOptIn[orgId] ?? false;\n  }\n  return false;\n}\n\nfunction getTeamIdsToQuery(teamId: number, parentOrgId: number | null | undefined): number[] {\n  if (parentOrgId) {\n    return [teamId, parentOrgId];\n  }\n  return [teamId];\n}\n\nfunction getOrgStateForTeam(\n  parentOrgId: number | null | undefined,\n  teamStates: Record<string, Record<number, FeatureState>>,\n  slug: FeatureId\n): FeatureState {\n  if (parentOrgId) {\n    return teamStates[slug]?.[parentOrgId] ?? \"inherit\";\n  }\n  return \"inherit\";\n}\n\n/**\n * Service class for managing feature opt-in logic.\n * Computes effective states based on global, org, team, and user settings.\n */\nexport class FeatureOptInService implements IFeatureOptInService {\n  constructor(private featuresRepository: FeaturesRepository) {}\n\n  /**\n   * Core method: Resolve feature states for a user across all their teams.\n   *\n   * Precedence rules:\n   * 1. Global disabled → false (stop)\n   * 2. Org explicitly disabled → false (stop)\n   * 3. Org explicitly enabled → allowed at org level, continue to teams\n   * 4. Org inherits (or no org) → check teams\n   * 5. All teams explicitly disabled → false (stop)\n   * 6. At least one team enabled OR inherits → allowed at team level, continue to user\n   * 7. User explicitly disabled → false\n   * 8. User explicitly enabled OR inherits → true\n   *\n   * Auto-opt-in transformation:\n   * - If autoOptInFeatures=true at a level AND state is \"inherit\", transform to \"enabled\"\n   * - This transformation happens before computing effectiveEnabled\n   */\n  async resolveFeatureStatesAcrossTeams({\n    userId,\n    orgId,\n    teamIds,\n    featureIds,\n  }: {\n    userId: number;\n    orgId: number | null;\n    teamIds: number[];\n    featureIds: FeatureId[];\n  }): Promise<Record<string, ResolvedFeatureState>> {\n    const allTeamIds = orgId !== null ? [orgId, ...teamIds] : teamIds;\n\n    const [allFeatures, allTeamStates, userStates, userAutoOptIn, teamsAutoOptIn] = await Promise.all([\n      this.featuresRepository.getAllFeatures(),\n      this.featuresRepository.getTeamsFeatureStates({ teamIds: allTeamIds, featureIds }),\n      this.featuresRepository.getUserFeatureStates({ userId, featureIds }),\n      this.featuresRepository.getUserAutoOptIn(userId),\n      this.featuresRepository.getTeamsAutoOptIn(allTeamIds),\n    ]);\n\n    const globalEnabledMap = new Map(allFeatures.map((feature) => [feature.slug, feature.enabled ?? false]));\n    const resolvedStates: Record<string, ResolvedFeatureState> = {};\n\n    for (const featureId of featureIds) {\n      const state = this.resolveFeatureState(\n        featureId,\n        orgId,\n        teamIds,\n        globalEnabledMap,\n        allTeamStates,\n        userStates,\n        teamsAutoOptIn,\n        userAutoOptIn\n      );\n      resolvedStates[featureId] = state;\n    }\n\n    return resolvedStates;\n  }\n\n  private resolveFeatureState(\n    featureId: FeatureId,\n    orgId: number | null,\n    teamIds: number[],\n    globalEnabledMap: Map<string, boolean>,\n    allTeamStates: Record<string, Record<number, FeatureState>>,\n    userStates: Record<string, FeatureState>,\n    teamsAutoOptIn: Record<number, boolean>,\n    userAutoOptIn: boolean\n  ): ResolvedFeatureState {\n    const globalEnabled = globalEnabledMap.get(featureId) ?? false;\n    const teamStatesById = allTeamStates[featureId] ?? {};\n\n    const orgState = getOrgState(orgId, teamStatesById);\n    const teamStates = teamIds.map((teamId) => teamStatesById[teamId] ?? \"inherit\");\n    const userState = userStates[featureId] ?? \"inherit\";\n    const orgAutoOptIn = getOrgAutoOptIn(orgId, teamsAutoOptIn);\n    const teamAutoOptIns = teamIds.map((teamId) => teamsAutoOptIn[teamId] ?? false);\n\n    const { effectiveOrgState, effectiveTeamStates, effectiveUserState } = applyAutoOptIn({\n      orgState,\n      teamStates,\n      userState,\n      orgAutoOptIn,\n      teamAutoOptIns,\n      userAutoOptIn,\n    });\n\n    const policy: OptInFeaturePolicy = \"permissive\";\n\n    const { enabled: effectiveEnabled, reason: effectiveReason } = computeEffectiveStateAcrossTeams({\n      globalEnabled,\n      orgState: effectiveOrgState,\n      teamStates: effectiveTeamStates,\n      userState: effectiveUserState,\n      policy,\n    });\n\n    return {\n      featureId,\n      globalEnabled,\n      orgState,\n      teamStates,\n      userState,\n      effectiveEnabled,\n      effectiveReason,\n      orgAutoOptIn,\n      teamAutoOptIns,\n      userAutoOptIn,\n    };\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * List all opt-in features with their states for a user across teams.\n   * Only returns features that are in the allowlist, globally enabled, and scoped to \"user\".\n   */\n  async listFeaturesForUser(input: {\n    userId: number;\n    orgId: number | null;\n    teamIds: number[];\n  }): Promise<ListFeaturesForUserResult[]> {\n    const { userId, orgId, teamIds } = input;\n    const userScopedFeatures = getOptInFeaturesForScope(\"user\");\n    const featureIds = userScopedFeatures.map((config) => config.slug);\n\n    const resolvedStates = await this.resolveFeatureStatesAcrossTeams({\n      userId,\n      orgId,\n      teamIds,\n      featureIds,\n    });\n\n    return featureIds.map((featureId) => resolvedStates[featureId]).filter((state) => !state.globalEnabled);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * List all opt-in features with their raw states for a team or organization.\n   * Used for team/org admin settings page to configure feature opt-in.\n   * Only returns features that are in the allowlist, globally enabled, and scoped to the specified scope.\n   * If parentOrgId is provided, also returns the organization state for each feature.\n   */\n  async listFeaturesForTeam(input: {\n    teamId: number;\n    parentOrgId?: number | null;\n    scope?: OptInFeatureScope;\n  }): Promise<ListFeaturesForTeamResult[]> {\n    const { teamId, parentOrgId, scope = \"team\" } = input;\n    const teamIdsToQuery = getTeamIdsToQuery(teamId, parentOrgId);\n    const scopedFeatures = getOptInFeaturesForScope(scope);\n\n    const [allFeatures, teamStates] = await Promise.all([\n      this.featuresRepository.getAllFeatures(),\n      this.featuresRepository.getTeamsFeatureStates({\n        teamIds: teamIdsToQuery,\n        featureIds: scopedFeatures.map((config) => config.slug),\n      }),\n    ]);\n\n    const results = scopedFeatures.map((config) => {\n      const globalFeature = allFeatures.find((f) => f.slug === config.slug);\n      const globalEnabled = globalFeature?.enabled ?? false;\n      const teamState = teamStates[config.slug]?.[teamId] ?? \"inherit\";\n      const orgState = getOrgStateForTeam(parentOrgId, teamStates, config.slug);\n\n      return { featureId: config.slug, globalEnabled, teamState, orgState };\n    });\n\n    return results.filter((result) => result.globalEnabled);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Set user's feature state.\n   * Delegates to FeaturesRepository.setUserFeatureState.\n   * Throws an error if the feature is not scoped to \"user\".\n   */\n  async setUserFeatureState(\n    input:\n      | { userId: number; featureId: FeatureId; state: \"enabled\" | \"disabled\"; assignedBy: number }\n      | { userId: number; featureId: FeatureId; state: \"inherit\" }\n  ): Promise<void> {\n    const { userId, featureId, state } = input;\n\n    if (!isFeatureAllowedForScope(featureId, \"user\")) {\n      throw new ErrorWithCode(\n        ErrorCode.BadRequest,\n        `Feature \"${featureId}\" is not available at the user scope`\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (state === \"inherit\") {\n      await this.featuresRepository.setUserFeatureState({ userId, featureId, state });\n    } else {\n      const { assignedBy } = input;\n      await this.featuresRepository.setUserFeatureState({\n        userId,\n        featureId,\n        state,\n        assignedBy: `user-${assignedBy}`,\n      });\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Set team's feature state.\n   * Delegates to FeaturesRepository.setTeamFeatureState.\n   * Throws an error if the feature is not scoped to the specified scope.\n   */\n  async setTeamFeatureState(\n    input:\n      | { teamId: number; featureId: FeatureId; state: \"enabled\" | \"disabled\"; assignedBy: number; scope?: OptInFeatureScope }\n      | { teamId: number; featureId: FeatureId; state: \"inherit\"; scope?: OptInFeatureScope }\n  ): Promise<void> {\n    const { teamId, featureId, state } = input;\n    const scope = input.scope ?? \"team\";\n\n    if (!isFeatureAllowedForScope(featureId, scope)) {\n      throw new ErrorWithCode(\n        ErrorCode.BadRequest,\n        `Feature \"${featureId}\" is not available at the ${scope} scope`\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (state === \"inherit\") {\n      await this.featuresRepository.setTeamFeatureState({ teamId, featureId, state });\n    } else {\n      const { assignedBy } = input;\n      await this.featuresRepository.setTeamFeatureState({\n        teamId,\n        featureId,\n        state,\n        assignedBy: `user-${assignedBy}`,\n      });\n    }\n  }\n}\n","numLines":308,"startLine":1,"totalLines":308}}}

type { ZodEnum } from \"zod\";\nimport { z } from \"zod\";\n\nimport { getFeatureOptInService } from \"@calcom/features/di/containers/FeatureOptInService\";\nimport { TeamRepository } from \"@calcom/features/ee/teams/repositories/TeamRepository\";\nimport { isOptInFeature } from \"@calcom/features/feature-opt-in/config\";\nimport { FeaturesRepository } from \"@calcom/features/flags/features.repository\";\nimport { MembershipRepository } from \"@calcom/features/membership/repositories/MembershipRepository\";\nimport { prisma } from \"@calcom/prisma\";\n\nimport { TRPCError } from \"@trpc/server\";\n\nimport authedProcedure from \"../../../procedures/authedProcedure\";\nimport { router } from \"../../../trpc\";\nimport { createOrgPbacProcedure, createTeamPbacProcedure } from \"../../../procedures/pbacProcedures\";\n\nconst featureStateSchema: ZodEnum<[\"enabled\", \"disabled\", \"inherit\"]> = z.enum([\"enabled\", \"disabled\", \"inherit\"]);\n\nconst featureOptInService = getFeatureOptInService();\nconst featuresRepository = new FeaturesRepository(prisma);\nconst teamRepository = new TeamRepository(prisma);\n\n/**\n * Helper to get user's org and team IDs from their memberships.\n * Returns orgId (if user belongs to an org) and teamIds (non-org teams).\n */\nasync function getUserOrgAndTeamIds(userId: number): Promise<{ orgId: number | null; teamIds: number[] }> {\n  const memberships = await MembershipRepository.findAllByUserId({\n    userId,\n    filters: { accepted: true },\n  });\n\n  let orgId: number | null = null;\n  const teamIds: number[] = [];\n\n  for (const membership of memberships) {\n    if (membership.team.isOrganization) {\n      orgId = membership.teamId;\n    } else {\n      teamIds.push(membership.teamId);\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { orgId, teamIds };\n}\n\nexport const featureOptInRouter = router({\n  /**\n   * Get all opt-in features with states for current user.\n   * This considers all teams/orgs the user belongs to.\n   */\n  listForUser: authedProcedure.query(async ({ ctx }) => {\n    const { orgId, teamIds } = await getUserOrgAndTeamIds(ctx.user.id);\n\n    return featureOptInService.listFeaturesForUser({\n      userId: ctx.user.id,\n      orgId,\n      teamIds,\n    });\n  }),\n\n  /**\n   * Get all opt-in features with states for a team settings page.\n   * Used by team admins to configure feature opt-in for their team.\n   * Also returns the organization state if the team belongs to an organization.\n   */\n  listForTeam: createTeamPbacProcedure(\"featureOptIn.read\").query(async ({ input }) => {\n    // Get the team's parent organization ID (if any)\n    const parentOrg = await teamRepository.findParentOrganizationByTeamId(input.teamId);\n    const parentOrgId = parentOrg?.id ?? null;\n\n    return featureOptInService.listFeaturesForTeam({ teamId: input.teamId, parentOrgId, scope: \"team\" });\n  }),\n\n  /**\n   * Get all opt-in features with states for organization settings page.\n   * Used by org admins to configure feature opt-in for their organization.\n   */\n  listForOrganization: createOrgPbacProcedure(\"featureOptIn.read\").query(async ({ ctx }) => {\n    // Organizations use the same listFeaturesForTeam since they're stored in TeamFeatures\n    // Pass scope: \"org\" to filter features that are scoped to organizations\n    return featureOptInService.listFeaturesForTeam({ teamId: ctx.organizationId, scope: \"org\" });\n  }),\n\n  /**\n   * Set user's feature state.\n   */\n  setUserState: authedProcedure\n    .input(\n      z.object({\n        slug: z.string(),\n        state: featureStateSchema,\n      })\n    )\n    .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {\n      await featureOptInService.setUserFeatureState({\n        userId: ctx.user.id,\n        featureId: input.slug,\n        state: input.state,\n        assignedBy: ctx.user.id,\n      });\n\n      return { success: true };\n    }),\n\n  /**\n   * Set team's feature state (requires team admin).\n   */\n  setTeamState: createTeamPbacProcedure(\"featureOptIn.update\")\n    .input(\n      z.object({\n        slug: z.string(),\n        state: featureStateSchema,\n      })\n    )\n    .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {\n      if (!isOptInFeature(input.slug)) {\n        throw new TRPCError({\n          code: \"BAD_REQUEST\",\n          message: \"Invalid slug. This feature is not opt-in configurable.\",\n        });\n      }\n\n      await featureOptInService.setTeamFeatureState({\n        teamId: input.teamId,\n        featureId: input.slug,\n        state: input.state,\n        assignedBy: ctx.user.id,\n        scope: \"team\",\n      });\n\n      return { success: true };\n    }),\n\n  /**\n   * Set organization's feature state (requires org admin).\n   */\n  setOrganizationState: createOrgPbacProcedure(\"featureOptIn.update\")\n    .input(\n      z.object({\n        slug: z.string(),\n        state: featureStateSchema,\n      })\n    )\n    .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {\n      if (!isOptInFeature(input.slug)) {\n        throw new TRPCError({\n          code: \"BAD_REQUEST\",\n          message: \"Invalid slug. This feature is not opt-in configurable.\",\n        });\n      }\n\n      // Organizations use the same TeamFeatures table\n      await featureOptInService.setTeamFeatureState({\n        teamId: ctx.organizationId,\n        featureId: input.slug,\n        state: input.state,\n        assignedBy: ctx.user.id,\n        scope: \"org\",\n      });\n\n      return { success: true };\n    }),\n\n  /**\n   * Get user's auto opt-in preference.\n   */\n  getUserAutoOptIn: authedProcedure.query(async ({ ctx }) => {\n    const autoOptIn = await featuresRepository.getUserAutoOptIn(ctx.user.id);\n    return { autoOptIn };\n  }),\n\n  /**\n   * Set user's auto opt-in preference.\n   */\n  setUserAutoOptIn: authedProcedure\n    .input(\n      z.object({\n        autoOptIn: z.boolean(),\n      })\n    )\n    .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {\n      await featuresRepository.setUserAutoOptIn(ctx.user.id, input.autoOptIn);\n      return { success: true };\n    }),\n\n  /**\n   * Get team's auto opt-in preference (requires team admin).\n   */\n  getTeamAutoOptIn: createTeamPbacProcedure(\"featureOptIn.read\").query(async ({ input }) => {\n    const result = await featuresRepository.getTeamsAutoOptIn([input.teamId]);\n    return { autoOptIn: result[input.teamId] ?? false };\n  }),\n\n  /**\n   * Set team's auto opt-in preference (requires team admin).\n   */\n  setTeamAutoOptIn: createTeamPbacProcedure(\"featureOptIn.update\")\n    .input(\n      z.object({\n        autoOptIn: z.boolean(),\n      })\n    )\n    .mutation(async ({ input }) => {\n      await featuresRepository.setTeamAutoOptIn(input.teamId, input.autoOptIn);\n      return { success: true };\n    }),\n\n  /**\n   * Get organization's auto opt-in preference (requires org admin).\n   */\n  getOrganizationAutoOptIn: createOrgPbacProcedure(\"featureOptIn.read\").query(async ({ ctx }) => {\n    const result = await featuresRepository.getTeamsAutoOptIn([ctx.organizationId]);\n    return { autoOptIn: result[ctx.organizationId] ?? false };\n  }),\n\n  /**\n   * Set organization's auto opt-in preference (requires org admin).\n   */\n  setOrganizationAutoOptIn: createOrgPbacProcedure(\"featureOptIn.update\")\n    .input(\n      z.object({\n        autoOptIn: z.boolean(),\n      })\n    )\n    .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {\n      await featuresRepository.setTeamAutoOptIn(ctx.organizationId, input.autoOptIn);\n      return { success: true };\n    }),\n});\n","numLines":231,"startLine":1,"totalLines":231}}}

But the current code will throw an error if `config.scope` is undefined.\n\n**Current (buggy):**\n```typescript\nreturn config.scope.includes(scope);\n```\n\n**Fixed:**\n```typescript\nreturn !config.scope || config.scope.includes(scope);\n```\n\n### 2. **FeatureOptInService.ts line 161** - HIGH: Hardcoded policy instead of using feature configuration\nThe `policy` variable is hardcoded to \"permissive\" instead of using the actual policy from the feature's configuration. This is a compliance violation - the configuration defines a policy per feature, but it's being ignored.\n\n**Current (buggy):**\n```typescript\nconst policy: OptInFeaturePolicy = \"permissive\";\n```\n\n**Fixed:** Get the policy from the feature config:\n```typescript\nconst config = getOptInFeatureConfig(featureId);\nconst policy: OptInFeaturePolicy = config?.policy ?? \"permissive\";\n```\n\n### 3. **FeatureOptInService.ts line 187-205** - MEDIUM: Comment contradicts implementation\nThe comment on line 187 says \"globally enabled\" but the filter on line 205 is `!state.globalEnabled` (globally disabled). For an opt-in system, features shown should be globally disabled (so users can opt in), making the logic correct but the comment wrong. This is a compliance violation.\n\n**Current comment (wrong):**\n```typescript\n * Only returns features that are in the allowlist, globally enabled, and scoped to \"user\".\n\n\n```\n\n**Fixed comment:**\n```typescript\n * Only returns features that are in the allowlist, globally disabled, and scoped to \"user\".\n```\n\n### 4. **_router.ts line 88-104** - HIGH: Missing slug validation in setUserState\nThe `setUserState` endpoint doesn't validate that the input slug is a valid opt-in feature, while `setTeamState` and `setOrganizationState` do. This is a compliance violation - inconsistent validation across endpoints.\n\n**Current (missing validation):**\nThe endpoint directly calls the service without validation like `setTeamState` and `setOrganizationState` do.\n\n**Fixed:**\nAdd validation before the service call:\n```typescript\nif (!isOptInFeature(input.slug)) {\n  throw new TRPCError({\n    code: \"BAD_REQUEST\",\n    message: \"Invalid slug. This feature is not opt-in configurable.\",\n  });\n}\n```\n\nThis ensures the user state endpoint enforces the same slug validation checks as the team and organization endpoints, maintaining consistency across all three endpoints.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":13,"cache_creation_input_tokens":10583,"cache_read_input_tokens":30287,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":10583},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f9ff3b5b-9f3c-4d46-a962-edfd64b40be7","uuid":"a342aa76-d9a7-4430-8640-58f5769956ae"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_017HaDfsxdccQBwPUQ3jDt6f","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01UpxAoeYQaKugApfmA9dp9H","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/review.json","content":"[\n  {\n    \"file\": \"packages/features/feature-opt-in/config.ts\",\n    \"line\": 76,\n    \"severity\": \"critical\",\n    \"description\": \"The isFeatureAllowedForScope function assumes config.scope is always defined, but the interface marks it as optional (line 10). When a feature has no scope field, calling config.scope.includes(scope) will throw a TypeError. According to the function's own documentation (lines 70-72), features without a scope field should be allowed for all scopes, but the code will crash instead of returning true.\",\n    \"fix_patch\": \"--- a/packages/features/feature-opt-in/config.ts\\n+++ b/packages/features/feature-opt-in/config.ts\\n@@ -73,7 +73,7 @@ export function getOptInFeaturesForScope(scope: OptInFeatureScope): OptInFeature\\n export function isFeatureAllowedForScope(slug: string, scope: OptInFeatureScope): boolean {\\n   const config = getOptInFeatureConfig(slug);\\n   if (!config) return false;\\n-  return config.scope.includes(scope);\\n+  return !config.scope || config.scope.includes(scope);\\n }\\n\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"file\": \"packages/features/feature-opt-in/services/FeatureOptInService.ts\",\n    \"line\": 161,\n    \"severity\": \"critical\",\n    \"description\": \"The resolveFeatureState method hardcodes the feature policy to 'permissive' instead of retrieving it from the feature configuration. This violates the compliance pattern established in the config module where each feature defines its own policy. Features with 'restrictive' policies will be evaluated with 'permissive' logic, causing incorrect behavior.\",\n    \"fix_patch\": \"--- a/packages/features/feature-opt-in/services/FeatureOptInService.ts\\n+++ b/packages/features/feature-opt-in/services/FeatureOptInService.ts\\n@@ -158,7 +158,8 @@ export class FeatureOptInService implements IFeatureOptInService {\\n       userAutoOptIn,\\n     });\\n \\n-    const policy: OptInFeaturePolicy = \\\"permissive\\\";\\n+    const config = getOptInFeatureConfig(featureId);\\n+    const policy: OptInFeaturePolicy = config?.policy ?? \\\"permissive\\\";\\n \\n     const { enabled: effectiveEnabled, reason: effectiveReason } = computeEffectiveStateAcrossTeams({\\n       globalEnabled,\\n\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"file\": \"packages/features/feature-opt-in/services/FeatureOptInService.ts\",\n    \"line\": 187,\n    \"severity\": \"medium\",\n    \"description\": \"The JSDoc comment at line 187 states the function 'Only returns features that are in the allowlist, globally enabled, and scoped to \\\"user\\\"', but the filter at line 205 is '!state.globalEnabled', which returns globally DISABLED features. For an opt-in system, this logic is correct (users opt into disabled features), but the comment is misleading and contradicts the implementation, violating documentation compliance.\",\n    \"fix_patch\": \"--- a/packages/features/feature-opt-in/services/FeatureOptInService.ts\\n+++ b/packages/features/feature-opt-in/services/FeatureOptInService.ts\\n@@ -185,7 +185,7 @@ export class FeatureOptInService implements IFeatureOptInService {\\n   /**\\n    * List all opt-in features with their states for a user across teams.\\n-   * Only returns features that are in the allowlist, globally enabled, and scoped to \\\"user\\\".\\n+   * Only returns features that are in the allowlist, globally disabled, and scoped to \\\"user\\\".\\n    */\\n   async listFeaturesForUser(input: {\\n     userId: number;\\n\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"file\": \"packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/featureOptIn/_router.ts\",\n    \"line\": 88,\n    \"severity\": \"high\",\n    \"description\": \"The setUserState endpoint does not validate that input.slug is a valid opt-in feature, unlike setTeamState (line 117) and setOrganizationState (line 146) which both call isOptInFeature(). This inconsistency violates the compliance pattern of slug validation across all mutation endpoints and allows invalid slugs to reach the service layer without early validation.\",\n    \"fix_patch\": \"--- a/packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/featureOptIn/_router.ts\\n+++ b/packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/featureOptIn/_router.ts\\n@@ -93,6 +93,12 @@ export const featureOptInRouter = router({\\n       })\\n     )\\n     .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {\\n+      if (!isOptInFeature(input.slug)) {\\n+        throw new TRPCError({\\n+          code: \\\"BAD_REQUEST\\\",\\n+          message: \\\"Invalid slug. This feature is not opt-in configurable.\\\",\\n+        });\\n+      }\\n       await featureOptInService.setUserFeatureState({\\n         userId: ctx.user.id,\\n         featureId: input.slug,\\n\"\n  }\n]\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":13,"cache_creation_input_tokens":10583,"cache_read_input_tokens":30287,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":10583},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f9ff3b5b-9f3c-4d46-a962-edfd64b40be7","uuid":"10b9bdd2-f8c5-479a-8b55-f5bfbdbc183f"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_017tZxyLmhFC9YEkffTHQLSu","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Perfect! I've created the review.json file with all four defects identified:\n\n1. **config.ts line 76** - CRITICAL: Missing null check for optional scope field\n   - Will throw TypeError when config.scope is undefined\n   - Fix: Add `!config.scope ||` check\n\n2. **FeatureOptInService.ts line 161** - CRITICAL: Hardcoded policy instead of using config\n   - Feature policies defined in config are ignored\n   - Fix: Get policy from feature config with fallback to \"permissive\"\n\n3. **FeatureOptInService.ts line 187** - MEDIUM: Comment contradicts implementation  \n   - Comment says \"globally enabled\" but code filters for \"!globalEnabled\" (globally disabled)\n   - Fix: Update comment to say \"globally disabled\"\n\n4. **_router.ts line 88** - HIGH: Missing slug validation in setUserState\n   - Inconsistent with setTeamState and setOrganizationState which validate\n   - Fix: Add isOptInFeature validation before service call\n\nAll defects have been properly documented with:\n- File path\n- Approximate line number\n- Severity level\n- Description explaining what's wrong and why it's a defect\n- Unified diff patches showing the proposed fix\n\nThe evaluation system should be able to apply these patches and verify they resolve the issues.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":12,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2173,"cache_read_input_tokens":40870,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2173},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f9ff3b5b-9f3c-4d46-a962-edfd64b40be7","uuid":"ff6c4d70-d495-4186-9073-0bb5e0dd2426"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_017tZxyLmhFC9YEkffTHQLSu","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I've completed the code review and identified **4 defects** in the feature opt-in scope configuration PR:\n\n## Summary\n\n### 1. **CRITICAL - Missing null check in isFeatureAllowedForScope** (config.ts:76)\nThe function assumes `config.scope` is always defined, but the interface marks it as optional. This will throw a TypeError when a feature has no scope field. According to the documentation, features without a scope field should be allowed for all scopes.\n\n### 2. **CRITICAL - Hardcoded policy instead of using config** (FeatureOptInService.ts:161)\nThe `resolveFeatureState` method hardcodes the policy to \"permissive\" instead of retrieving it from the feature configuration. This causes all features to be evaluated with the wrong policy, breaking the compliance pattern where each feature defines its own policy.\n\n### 3. **MEDIUM - Comment contradicts implementation** (FeatureOptInService.ts:187)\nThe JSDoc comment claims the method returns \"globally enabled\" features, but the actual filter returns \"globally disabled\" features (opt-in features). While the logic is correct for an opt-in system, the misleading comment violates documentation compliance.\n\n### 4. **HIGH - Missing slug validation in setUserState** (_router.ts:88)\nThe `setUserState` endpoint doesn't validate the input slug against the opt-in allowlist, unlike `setTeamState` and `setOrganizationState`. This creates inconsistent validation patterns across mutation endpoints.\n\nAll defects have been documented with unified diff patches in `/workspace/review.json` ready for application by the evaluation system."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":12,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2173,"cache_read_input_tokens":40870,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2173},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f9ff3b5b-9f3c-4d46-a962-edfd64b40be7","uuid":"06ae263e-1ce4-4a0e-b67e-70847f50ecff"}
